pymdp
  • Algos
  • FPI (fixed-point iteration)
  • View page source

FPI (fixed-point iteration)

JAX implementations live in the monolithic module pymdp.algos (not pymdp.algos.fpi). These routines perform mean-field style fixed-point updates on marginal beliefs over hidden states, using likelihoods from generative model A tensors. The inference layer selects among FPI, MMP, and related paths when building agents.

NumPy-era counterparts live under pymdp.legacy.algos; this page documents the JAX API only.

See also

  • inference — high-level infer_states and dispatch

  • MMP (marginal message passing) — marginal message passing for sequence models

  • Repository tests: test/test_fpi.py

  • Sidecar index: docxology/docs/validation_matrix.md (state inference → MMP / FPI)

API reference

pymdp.algos.run_vanilla_fpi(A: list[Array], obs: list[Array], prior: list[Array], num_iter: int = 1, distr_obs: bool = True) → list[Array]

Vanilla fixed point iteration (jaxified)

pymdp.algos.run_factorized_fpi(A: list[Array], obs: list[Array], prior: list[Array], A_dependencies: list[list[int]], num_iter: int = 1, distr_obs: bool = True) → list[Array]

Run the fixed point iteration algorithm with sparse dependencies between factors and observations (stored in A_dependencies)

pymdp.algos.run_factorized_fpi_hybrid(log_likelihoods: list[Array], prior: list[Array], A_dependencies: list[list[int]], num_iter: int) → list[Array]
pymdp.algos.run_factorized_fpi_end2end_padded(lls_padded: Array, prior: list[Array], A_dependencies: list[list[int]], max_obs_dim: int, max_state_dim: int, num_iter: int) → list[Array]
